quantification.why: Comparisons are mostly bare revenue rankings ('Gadget led... $2,392.00', 'Widget followed at $2,118.94'), with only one computed comparison ('roughly 35% below North's $2,176.35') plus per-unit prices.

specificity.why: Every recommendation names a specific product/region with figures and an actionable move, e.g. 'Sprocket sold 238 units yet earned just $1,130.50; consider raising its $4.75 price or bundling.'

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations—Sprocket's highest volume (238 units) yet lowest revenue, Gadget's high revenue from just 16 units, and West being 'the only region below $1,500'—beyond restating totals.

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. West is "$1,414.82, roughly 35% below North's $2,176.35" and Sprocket's "238 units... at only $4.75 each."

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a specific product/region with figures, e.g. "Sprocket sold 238 units yet earned just $1,130.50; consider raising its $4.75 price or bundling it."

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations like Gadget's high revenue from "just 16 units" versus Sprocket's inverse of "238 units... ranked last with $1,130.50."

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. 'West trails all regions at $1,414.82, roughly 35% below North's $2,176.35' plus per-unit prices ($4.75, $149.50) and unit counts throughout.

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a specific product/region with figures and an actionable move, e.g. 'Sprocket sold 238 units yet earned just $1,130.50; consider raising its $4.75 price or bundling it with higher-margin products.'

insight_density.why: Multiple non-obvious, actionable observations surface, such as Gadget's high revenue from 'just 16 units' at $149.50 and Sprocket ranking 'last' despite 'by far the highest volume' at 238 units.
